results.title: The "Flolan Rash": An Impressively Asymptomatic and Non-Inflammatory Eruption Following Long-Term Epoprostenol Therapy.
results.abstract: Epoprostenol (Flolan) is a last-resort intravenous medication for the treatment of severe pulmonary arterial hypertension (PAH). Cutaneous adverse events of Flolan are well-known by pulmonologists, though lacking in dermatologic literature. We report a near erythrodermic appearing, yet asymptomatic eruption lasting 10 years in a woman with end-stage PAH treated with long-term intravenous epoprostenol. Non-pruritic, blanching, erythematous papules coalescing to plaques surrounded by a hypopigmented halo encompassed her entire torso, as well as bilateral upper and lower extremities. Additional findings included bright red palms and soles associated with pain and tingling while walking. Laboratory workup revealed thrombocytopenia and a slightly elevated erythrocyte sedimentation rate (ESR); connective tissue disease markers were negative. Skin biopsies were, surprisingly, largely unremarkable without an inflammatory infiltrate. The patient was trialed on topical clobetasol ointment withou
results.journal: Journal of drugs in dermatology : JDD

results.title: Characteristics of hospitalizations and emergency department visits due to generalized pustular psoriasis in the United States.
results.abstract: Generalized pustular psoriasis (GPP) is a rare and severe, inflammatory skin disease. GPP is characterized by recurrent flares that consist of disseminated erythematous skin rash with sterile neutrophil-filled pustules that can result in an emergency department (ED) visit or hospital stay due to systemic complications. This study characterizes hospitalizations, ED visits, and inpatient treatment due to GPP in the United States (US).

results.title: Multiple eruptive cutaneous horns on the scalp.
Gianotti-Crosti syndrome in two adult patients.
results.abstract: Gianotti-Crosti syndrome was first described in Italy by Gianotti in 1955. It is considered a benign, self-limited exanthem that occurs in younger children. Adult cases are rare.
results.journal: Journal of cutaneous medicine and surgery
